title: The lie I believe about myself
subtitle: A journal prompt for finding the core negative belief you carry and examining it
excerpt: A journal prompt for finding the belief about yourself that most limits you. Not the surface self-criticism. The deeper one that shapes your decisions before you realise it.

practice.whenToUse: When patterns of self-sabotage, avoidance, or limitation keep repeating and you want to find what belief is running underneath them

content.text: Most people have one core belief about themselves that they don't say out loud. They might say I'm not good enough or I always get it wrong in low moments, but the deeper version is more specific. This prompt tries to find it.
What is the self-critical thought you return to most? What do you say to yourself when something goes wrong?
What do you assume about yourself in situations where you feel insecure? What does the insecurity claim is true?
Complete this sentence: The thing I most believe about myself that limits me is __. Now write one page on where that belief came from. Not whether it is true. Where it came from.
What would be different in your life if that belief were not there? What would you try? What would you stop avoiding?
Is there any evidence against it? Not reassurance. Actual evidence from your actual life.
